codeReview.summary: I reviewed the original failing feature diff (e9668307c904b45623a4326498d256cfd1888cc8) and the fix diff (6adcd5b764a220c485af31c452abb3d886e40b0d), along with the fix worker transcript skeleton and handoff. The prior blocker was that `OpenAITTSModel::synthesize()` built request state but discarded it, so runtime output never reflected configured model/voice/speed. The fix resolves that gap by deriving the emitted runtime audio payload from the built request (`crates/agents-voice/src/models/openai_tts.rs:41-62`) and by adding focused unit and facade regressions (`crates/agents-voice/src/models/openai_tts.rs:110-141`, `crates/openai-agents/tests/voice_semantics.rs:210-239`) that would fail if runtime forwarding regressed. This adequately addresses the original TTS-specific blocking finding; the unrelated STT blockers from the prior review remain outside this fix feature's scope.

summary: Pass. The rerun fix closes the prior blocking TTS runtime-forwarding defect by making the real Rust synthesize path consume the concrete request settings and by adding executable regressions at both the model and facade pipeline layers. Report written for the scrutiny validator.
